programs.name: Gymnastics - Advanced
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. With a focus on strength training and technique, you will work on front handsprings, safe dismounts and how to connect movements to create tumbling passes on various apparatuses. Prerequisite: Gymnastics (Intermediate).
programs.location: Joseph J. Piccininni Cmty Rec Centre

programs.name: Gymnastics - Advanced
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. With a focus on strength training and technique, you will work on front handsprings, safe dismounts and how to connect movements to create tumbling passes on various apparatuses. Prerequisite: Gymnastics (Intermediate).
programs.location: Joseph J. Piccininni Cmty Rec Centre

programs.name: Gymnastics - Beginner
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all.  With a focus on safe landing techniques, you will learn and practice basic skills such as walks, jumps, rolls and turns on various apparatuses.
programs.location: Joseph J. Piccininni Cmty Rec Centre

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Recreational sport programs provide an opportunity for participants who have some level of skill in a specific sport to participate in structured scrimmage or small sided games.  No instruction is provided.   This program is supervised by staff.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills though play with recreational pickleball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills though play with recreational pickleball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Pilates: Chair
programs.description: Gain all the benefits of pilates with the added support of a chair or your walker. Build core strength and improve flexibility with this workout. As you progress through slow-paced movements, performed in sequence, you will learn to incorporate an awareness of the spine and breath in this complete approach to well being.

programs.name: Pilates: Chair
programs.description: Gain all the benefits of pilates with the added support of a chair or your walker. Build core strength and improve flexibility with this workout. As you progress through slow-paced movements, performed in sequence, you will learn to incorporate an awareness of the spine and breath in this complete approach to well being.

programs.name: Soccer: House League
programs.description: Be part of a team! Our soccer house league activity is structured to teach you the basic skills and rules of the game, and then put them into action through recreational level competitive play. As you learn and develop soccer fundamentals, you will also learn the importance of teamwork and respectful play. Game times are built into scheduled classes, no additional days/times are required.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Philpott Tennis
programs.description: Free tennis  instructions available for  beginner and intermediate participants ages 6-7. Offered at various sites in  priority neighborhoods across the city. The program is run by qualified  instructors hired and supervised by Philpott Children’s Tennis and all  equipment is provided. Participants will use 21-inch racquets, red balls and  play on ½ court. Philpott Children’s Tennis teaches their core values to  participants to help encourage them in their challenges of sport and life. Thanks  to the generous support of Philpott Children’s Tennis and its donors.

Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Aquatic Games and Skills
programs.description: Playing games and having fun is a wonderful way to practice swimming skills. Designed for swimmers with a disability or special needs, structured aquatic games and activities such as water polo, synchronized swimming and aquatic fitness, provide the perfect oppourtunity to learn and build on basic swimming fundamentals.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Hockey Skills

Learn puck and stick handling, passing and shooting, as well as game strategy through a combination of lessons, drills and play.Prerequisite: Successful completion of Learn to Skate 1 or Learn to Skate - Beginner or able to skate one length of the ice surface.Mandatory Equipment: Skaters must bring (wear) a CSA-approved hockey helmet with a full visor or cage, a hockey stick, hockey skates, hockey gloves and a neck guard.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Arthritis
programs.description: Enjoy a work out in the water to improve your fitness and reduce stress on your joints. Designed for participants with arthritis and joint injuries, movements will focus on improving range of motion through muscle conditioning, flexibility and strength-building exercises.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Swim and Lifesaving Instructor
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. Participants learn to organize, plan, teach and evaluate swimming, lifesaving and first aid skills. Discover and develop skills in the areas of class management, teaching, evaluation, leadership, communication and administration. With the successful completion of this course, candidates will be qualified to instruct the Toronto Learn to Swim program, the Lifesaving Society Swim for Life program, Emergency First Aid with CPR-B, Rookie, Ranger and Star Patrol as well as Bronze Star, Medallion and Cross.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old and Bronze Cross (current or expired).For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Workshop: Hammered Flower Prints
programs.description: Are you looking for focused classes with a shorter time commitment? Hammered flower prints workshop, join us for a fun and educational workshop where you will Join us on Toronto Island to create colourful flower collage art,  in the enchanting Franklin Children's Garden. Pick your favourite flowers in  the garden and transfer their beautiful colours and shapes onto paper to make a  unique piece of art. Please arrive at the Jack Layton Ferry Terminal in time to take  the 9:00am ferry to Centre Island. Upon arrival at the Centre Island Ferry  Dock, our Garden Staff will meet you and accompany you on a short walk to  Franklin Children's Garden.
programs.location: Toronto Island Park - Franklin Children's Gardn

programs.name: Workshop: Sun Dyeing
programs.description: Are you looking for focused classes with a shorter time commitment? Sun Dyeing workshop, join us for a fun and educational workshop where you'll learn the  art of sun-dying using natural materials. Discover how to create images on  paper and fabric using plants as stencils. This hands-on course will allow  participants to create unique designs on 100% cotton material or paper using a  natural turmeric dye solution and the light from the Sun. Please arrive at the Jack Layton Ferry Terminal in time to take  the 9:00am ferry to Centre Island. Upon arrival at the Centre Island Ferry Dock,  our Garden Staff will meet you and accompany you on a short walk to Franklin Children's Garden. Note: In the event of an overcast or raint day, an alternate activity will be run.

Please arrive at the Jack Layton Ferry Terminal in time to take  the 9:00am ferry to Centre Island. Upon arrival at the Centre Island Ferry Dock,  our Garden Staff will meet you and accompany you on a short walk to Franklin Children's Garden. Note: In the event of an overcast or raint day, an alternate activity will be run.
programs.location: Toronto Island Park - Franklin Children's Gardn

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Ultra Swim - Beginner
programs.description: Swim at your own pace. Discover water movements through sensory cues such as look, listen and feel and practice fun ways to enter and exit the pool to gain comfort in the water. Learn essential swimming skills such as floating, kicking, and gliding and understand the importance of water safety and how to be safe in and around water environments. Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca. Please Note: External support persons attending with you shall enter water as required and can attend at no charge.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

Extended hours activities have a separate barcode for registration. Please note that campers must be registered at the same location for extended hours as the activity they have registered in. Refer to the camp program charts for times, fees and locations.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.description: Playing games and having fun is a wonderful way to practice swimming skills. Designed for swimmers with a disability or special needs, structured aquatic games and activities such as water polo, synchronized swimming and aquatic fitness, provide the perfect oppourtunity to learn and build on basic swimming fundamentals.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.
